steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher claims some lakes have seen an increase in ice duration over time. Supporting data must show a specific lake with more days of ice in 2005–06 than in 1980–81.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Lake Baikal had fewer days of ice in the winter of 1980-81 than it did in the winter of 2005-06.": Fewer days earlier, more days later — that is an increase over time, directly supporting the researcher's claim. ✓
  • "Kitusjärvi is at a higher latitude than Mirror Lake and typically had more days of ice per winter than Mirror Lake did.": Compares two different lakes to each other; says nothing about either lake's change over time. ✗
  • "Kitusjärvi had more days of ice in the winter of 1980-81 than it did in the winter of 2005-06.": More days earlier, fewer days later — a decrease, which is the opposite of what the researcher claims. ✗
  • "Kitusjärvi is at a higher latitude than Mirror Lake and typically had fewer days of ice per winter than Mirror Lake did.": Again compares two lakes to each other with no information about change over time. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
